color_semantics.TEAL: quantum correlation / CHSH score 2‚ąö2 / entangled singlet / quantum zone
color_semantics.CRIMSON: local hidden-variable / classical ceiling 2 / pre-set answers / Einstein picture

metadata.note: Card 09 ‚ÄĒ score 8/10 ‚ÄĒ scan move ‚ÄĒ exclusions: no full CHSH algebraic derivation, no loophole taxonomy, no interpretation debate, no Tsirelson-bound proof

beats.narration_text: Einstein believed particles carry hidden pre-set answers before measurement. That belief has a testable consequence ‚ÄĒ a number that cannot exceed 2.

card.headline: Why 2 Becomes 2‚ąö2 ‚ÄĒ How Bell Beat Einstein

beats.narration_text: Alice and Bob each choose between two spin-measurement axes. The CHSH score combines four correlations. Any world with pre-set answers: score at most 2. That is the classical ceiling.
beats.description: Two detector icons (Alice, Bob) each with two axis choices. Equation: |E(a‚āĀ,b‚āĀ)+E(a‚āĀ,b‚āā)+E(a‚āā,b‚āĀ)‚ąíE(a‚āā,b‚āā)| ‚Č§ 2. CRIMSON border around the bound '2'.

beats.narration_text: The question: if every experiment produces a score above 2, what does that mean for Einstein's picture?

card.headline: Can experiment push the score above 2?

beats.narration_text: For the singlet ‚ÄĒ two entangled spin-half particles ‚ÄĒ the quantum correlation between axes at angle őł is minus cosine őł. Choose the four axes at 0¬į, 45¬į, 90¬į, 135¬į and compute.
beats.description: Four angle labels: a‚āĀ=0¬į, b‚āĀ=45¬į, a‚āā=90¬į, b‚āā=135¬į. Correlation formula: E_QM = ‚ąícosőł in TEAL. Each pairwise angle difference labeled.

beats.narration_text: Each correlation is minus one over root 2. Combine four of them: the CHSH score is 2 root 2 ‚ÄĒ about 2.83. That is 41 percent above the classical ceiling.
beats.description: Step: each E = ‚ąí1/‚ąö2 ‚Čą ‚ąí0.707. CHSH sum = ‚ąí4/‚ąö2 = ‚ąí2‚ąö2. |S| = 2‚ąö2 ‚Čą 2.828. TEAL highlighted. Classical 2 shown below in CRIMSON. Gap labeled '41%'.

beats.narration_text: A classical correlated pair gives linear correlations. The singlet's quantum correlation follows minus cosine őł ‚ÄĒ a curve, not a line. The curve is more extreme near 45¬į, and that is exactly where CHSH probes it.
beats.description: Plot: x-axis angle őł from 0¬į to 180¬į. Classical (linear, CRIMSON dashed) vs quantum (‚ąícosőł curve, TEAL solid). At őł=45¬į gap is visible. CHSH probing angles marked with teal dots.

beats.narration_text: Aspect measured 2.697 in 1982. Loophole-free tests in 2015 confirmed 2.828 at ten standard deviations. Local hidden variables are ruled out, not just unlikely.
beats.description: Number line: 0 to 3. Marker at 2 (CRIMSON, 'LHV max'). Marker at 2‚ąö2‚Čą2.83 (TEAL, 'Tsirelson bound / quantum max'). Arrow at 2.697 labeled 'Aspect 1982'. Arrow at 2.828 labeled '2015 loophole-free'.

beats.narration_text: The Tsirelson bound shows 2 root 2 is the highest any quantum state can score ‚ÄĒ no quantum trick can go further. Between 2 and 2 root 2 sits everything quantum mechanics allows but local hidden variables forbid.

props.headline: The Tsirelson bound shows 2 root 2 is the highest any‚Ä¶

beats.still_prompt: B10, clean educational diagram: a horizontal number line from 0 to 3. At value 2: a red vertical bar labeled 'Classical ceiling (LHV ‚Č§ 2)'. At value 2.828: a teal vertical bar labeled 'Tsirelson / Quantum max (2‚ąö2)'. The region between 2 and 2.828 is shaded teal and labeled 'Quantum territory'. To the left of 2 is shaded crimson and labeled 'Classical territory'. Black background, white axis and text labels, clean physics illustration.

beats.narration_text: Entanglement does not allow faster-than-light signaling. Bob's marginal outcome is random regardless of Alice's choice. To see the correlation, you must compare data ‚ÄĒ and that comparison travels at light speed.
beats.description: Alice and Bob separated by large distance. Alice flips a coin choosing axis. Bob's outcome histogram: same random 50/50 either way. Labels: 'No FTL signal ‚ÄĒ marginal is random'. Only after classical comparison: correlation appears.

beats.narration_text: Singlet: E = ‚ąícosőł. Angles: a‚āĀ=0¬į, b‚āĀ=45¬į, a‚āā=90¬į, b‚āā=135¬į. E‚āĀ‚āĀ = ‚ąícos 45¬į = ‚ąí1/‚ąö2. E‚āĀ‚āā = ‚ąícos 45¬į = ‚ąí1/‚ąö2. E‚āā‚āĀ = ‚ąícos 45¬į = ‚ąí1/‚ąö2. E‚āā‚āā = ‚ąícos 135¬į = +1/‚ąö2. CHSH sum: 4 times 1/‚ąö2 equals 2‚ąö2.
beats.description: Four-row table: (a‚āĀ,b‚āĀ)=45¬į ‚Üí ‚ąí1/‚ąö2; (a‚āĀ,b‚āā)=45¬į ‚Üí ‚ąí1/‚ąö2; (a‚āā,b‚āĀ)=45¬į ‚Üí ‚ąí1/‚ąö2; (a‚āā,b‚āā)=135¬į ‚Üí +1/‚ąö2. Sum shown: |‚ąí1/‚ąö2 ‚ąí1/‚ąö2 ‚ąí1/‚ąö2 ‚ąí(+1/‚ąö2)| = 4/‚ąö2 = 2‚ąö2. Final box TEAL.

beats.narration_text: Can experiment push the score above 2? Yes ‚ÄĒ to exactly 2 root 2. That 41-percent gap is the experimental signature of quantum entanglement, ruling out every local pre-set-answer theory Einstein imagined.

card.headline:
Entanglement scores 2‚ąö2.
Pre-set answers are dead.
